title: Implement native Argus MCP facade for headless visual inspection and steering
operator_request: Create the Argus microtask and start implementing Argus as the named native visual inspection and GUI steering tool. It must be Rust-native, headless, non-intrusive, parallel-agent usable, and must never pop Handshake forward or steal keyboard/mouse/focus.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: If a no-context model resumes this MT, do not launch a foreground Handshake window. First inspect this contract, then inspect the native MCP files under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/mcp. Implement Argus as a named facade over the existing native AccessKit/MCP path, preserving the generic MCP tools. Use focused tests before product edits and do not touch inherited diagnostics/Palmistry work.

scope.summary: Add the first product-native Argus surface by naming and exposing Argus operations over the existing native MCP/AccessKit inspection and steering stack.
base_scope: Add a Rust-native `mcp::argus` facade that defines the canonical Argus method namespace and maps it to existing MCP primitives.
Expose model-callable Argus methods for inspect/list, click, set_value, and screenshot without removing the existing generic MCP methods.
Ensure Argus read operations use the existing shared snapshot lease and mutating operations use the existing per-target exclusive lease and attribution path.
Ensure Argus screenshot uses the existing focus-safe screenshot closure/path and does not call foreground, keyboard, mouse, or z-order APIs.
Return or preserve enough metadata for a model to recognize that the response came through Argus and what underlying primitive was used.
Add focused Rust tests that fail before implementation and pass after implementation for Argus method dispatch, session attribution/leases, and screenshot compatibility.
must_preserve: Existing MCP methods: list_widgets, click_widget, set_value, screenshot
McpSession lease registry and attributed action log
No foreground-window, keyboard-injection, OS mouse, SetForegroundWindow, BringWindowToTop, or z-order APIs
acceptance_criteria: Argus has a named Rust module or public API under the native MCP stack.
A model can call Argus inspect/list through JSON-RPC and receive the same UI tree snapshot shape as list_widgets, plus Argus-identifying metadata or method routing evidence.
A model can call Argus click and Argus set_value against stable author_id targets and the calls enqueue through the existing ActionChannel.
Argus mutating calls are leased and attributed the same way as click_widget/set_value, including agent_id stamping and action-log records.
Argus screenshot dispatches through the existing screenshot adapter/closure and returns VisualCaptureResult-compatible JSON.
Unknown/unauthorized Argus requests fail with typed JSON-RPC errors and do not take leases, log actions, or enqueue actions.
Focused tests prove the Argus aliases/facade are headless and do not require launching or foregrounding Handshake.
This MT does not claim full Argus visual-workbench parity; it only establishes the named native product surface that future MTs can extend.
The internal UserManual names the Argus MCP methods and the required inspect -> steer -> re-inspect -> screenshot evidence loop.
proof_targets: Failing test first for Argus inspect/list method routing.
Failing test first for Argus click session attribution/lease behavior.
Failing test first for Argus screenshot result compatibility.
Focused cargo test over the native MCP unit tests after implementation.
No new foreground-window or keyboard/mouse API usage introduced by diff review.

implementation_plan: 1. Add failing focused tests in the native MCP tool/session test modules for `argus.inspect`, `argus.click`, and `argus.screenshot`.
2. Add `src/mcp/argus.rs` with canonical Argus method constants and normalization from Argus method names to existing MCP primitives.
3. Update `mcp::tools::dispatch_request` to route Argus methods through the existing primitive handlers while preserving the response shape and adding minimal Argus metadata where safe.
4. Update `McpSession::decide` so Argus read/mutate methods take the same shared/exclusive leases as their primitive equivalents.
6. Run focused native MCP tests and record results in this MT.

commands_run.evidence: 4 Argus tests failed before implementation because argus.* methods returned unknown-method/null result paths.
6 passed, 0 failed: argus method map, argus.inspect, argus.click, argus.set_value, argus.screenshot, and argus click session lease/attribution.
Matches are comments in mcp::screenshot and mcp::mod documenting that those APIs are not called; no new foreground/input call site was introduced.
7 passed, 0 failed; Argus facade, routing metadata, session attribution/lease, and manual topic.
5 passed, 0 failed; TCP Argus inspect/click/set_value/screenshot, negative paths, lease timeout, parallel attribution.
1 passed, 0 failed; structured manual rows use real Argus tools.
1 passed, 0 failed; Argus topic includes method loop, agent_label, ActionLog, EventLedger/diagnostics posture.
PASS_WITH_COMMENT_MATCHES: matches are comments documenting forbidden APIs, no call sites.

results.summary: First native Argus MCP facade is implemented in product code and covered by focused headless tests.
No product commit has been made because the product worktree had pre-existing dirty changes in the same MCP files; stage hunks path-limited if committing this MT.
Wire-level TCP/named-pipe Argus alias tests are recommended as the next hardening slice; direct dispatch and session safety are covered here.
Transport/security reviewer PASS: one production token plus agent_label distinguishes parallel clients; unauthorized/unknown/malformed/lease timeout paths do not enqueue/log/retain leases.

Manual/spec reviewer PASS after remediation: internal UserManual includes Argus verification proof, MCP ActionLog, Flight Recorder/EventLedger posture, internal_diagnostics and Palmistry DEFERRED-with-reason.

manual_requirement: Update the in-product internal UserManual in the same implementation change so models and operators know Argus exists, which methods to call, and that missing Argus visibility/steerability is technical debt.

risk_notes.risk: Argus becomes a rename-only wrapper and models still cannot tell what path was used.
risk_notes.failure_scenario: A no-context model sees generic MCP responses and cannot cite Argus evidence.
risk_notes.mitigation: Expose canonical Argus method names and include Argus routing evidence in tests; add metadata only where it does not break existing clients.

risk_notes.risk: Adding Argus bypasses the existing lease/attribution path.
risk_notes.failure_scenario: Parallel agents can race on the same target using Argus aliases while generic MCP calls are protected.
risk_notes.mitigation: Route Argus methods through the same `McpSession::decide` classification and add tests proving agent_id/action-log behavior.

risk_notes.risk: Visual capture accidentally uses foreground desktop automation.
risk_notes.failure_scenario: Argus fixes inspection by popping Handshake forward or moving the operator mouse.
risk_notes.mitigation: Reuse `screenshot` closure/capture only; run grep for known foreground/input APIs and keep screenshot proof headless.

handoff.summary: Implemented: Rust-native Argus MCP facade with canonical argus.* methods, metadata stamping, same-token agent_label attribution, quiet/headless wire tests, and internal UserManual diagnostics posture. Awaiting validator.

risks: Alias methods can create security drift if auth checks are duplicated instead of reused.
Argus can be over-scoped into a full UI workbench before the first native command surface is stable.
A renamed method can miss the shared lease path and undermine parallel-agent safety.
minimum_controls: Reuse `dispatch_request` auth gate and existing `McpSession` safety state.
Keep MT-007 to named facade and proof tests only.
Test both direct dispatch and session dispatch paths.

adversarial_review.summary: Initial reviews found same-token attribution and manual diagnostics gaps; both remediated and re-reviewed PASS.
